title: The Reversal
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A respected psychiatrist begins to suspect that her newest patient is the one treating her, and that she has been the patient all along.
long_description:
Dr. Cassia Morrigan has spent fifteen years on her side of the chair. She is careful, competent, and respected. When a soft-spoken man named Henry Voss is referred to her for anxiety, she takes him on as a case.

From the first session, Henry seems to know things about Cassia that no patient should know. The name of a dog she had at nine. The corner of her mouth. The patient who once said, "A long walk." Her other patients begin to echo her own life back to her: a woman who describes Cassia's dead father, a teenage boy who watches her kitchen window, a man convinced he has been replaced.

The clinical boundary — the one that keeps the doctor on one side of the chair and the patient on the other — begins to dissolve. Notes appear in Cassia's own handwriting that she does not remember writing. Reflections in compact mirrors blink late. Drives home are gaps in her memory.

A quiet, spare psychological thriller about the line between healer and healed.
